title: Cinematic and Web Funnel Engine — seam-sequence.json (Skill 62, U14)
description: Input to scripts/verify_seams.py. An ORDERED list of clips (scenes and/or connectors, per spec Section 9.1's architectures A/B/C) whose adjacent boundary-frame continuity (ADR-9) must be checked. Each entry names a clip_id and the path to that clip's own extract_boundaries.py (U13) boundary-frames.json receipt -- that receipt must include BOTH 'first' and 'last' frame entries (extract_boundaries.py run with --position both, the default). The seam at sequence position i is clips[i]'s LAST frame vs clips[i+1]'s FIRST frame -- i.e. the actual encoded hand-off a browser will play across, not a provider preview or the original still (ADR-9).

clips.description: At least 2 clips are required for there to be any seam to verify.

boundary_frames_path.description: Path to a boundary-frames.json receipt written by scripts/extract_boundaries.py for this exact clip.

role.description: Optional bookkeeping only -- verify_seams.py treats every entry identically regardless of role.
